title: Contract violations become UB in release builds rather than hard assertions
description: Contracts (requires/ensures) are runtime checks in debug builds. In release builds they become 'optimizer assumptions' — if a contract is violated, the compiler assumes it cannot happen and the result is undefined behavior. This means contracts in release mode are not safety guarantees; they are performance hints. A caller who relies on a function's 'ensures' clause to know what they can trust gets UB instead of an error when the contract is false. Contracts must either remain as hard assertions in release mode (with a performance cost the caller can accept), or be clearly documented as debug-only assertions so callers know they provide zero runtime guarantee in production.
